hawkish_phrases: the case for an increase in the federal funds rate has strengthened
postponing policy firming for too long could push the unemployment rate markedly below its longer-run normal rate
three members preferred to raise the target range for the federal funds rate by 25 basis points at this meeting
could require the Committee to raise policy interest rates faster and more aggressively later on
waiting too long to resume the process of policy firming could pose risks to the economic expansion
dovish_phrases: with some slack likely remaining in the labor market and inflation continuing to run below the Committee's objective
decided, for the time being, to wait for further evidence of continued progress toward its objectives
inflation has continued to run below the Committee's 2 percent longer-run objective
few signs of emerging inflationary pressures or that progress on inflation had been slow
a cautious approach to removing accommodation was warranted given the proximity of policy rates to the effective lower bound
summary: The Committee voted 7-3 to hold rates at 1/4 to 1/2 percent while stating that the case for a rate increase had strengthened but decided to wait for further evidence, with three dissents from George, Mester, and Rosengren preferring an immediate 25bp hike.
